This rice variety is also commonly used to make rice dumplings, a popular snack in Thailand, thanks to its ability to hold its shape when molded and cooked. In addition to its exceptional taste and texture, Thai Glutinous Rice offers various nutritional benefits. It is a rich source of carbohydrates, providing the body with energy for optimal functioning. This type of rice also contains essential vitamins and minerals like manganese, which aids in energy metabolism and the formation of connective tissues. Furthermore, it contains small amounts of fiber, contributing to a healthy digestive system. Diammonium hydrogen phosphate is widely used in agriculture. It is used as a nitrogen and phosphorus fertilizer to supply nitrogen and phosphorus nutrients required by crops, promote plant growth, improve yield and quality. In addition, diammonium hydrogen phosphate can also be used to control phosphorus deficiency in soil, and can improve soil texture and fertility.
Diammonium Phosphate(DAP) application:
Phosphorus fertilizer is generally used as the base fertilizer. Under the same conditions, the higher the soil available phosphorus content, the lower the fertilizer efficiency. The suitable dosage range of phosphorus fertilizer for several main crops on the general phosphorus deficiency soil is: Phosphorus fertilizer (P2O5) (4-6) kg for grain crops, sweet potato, potato and cotton, peanut, rape, soybean, jute, tea tree (3-5) kg, watermelon (2-4) kg, tobacco (1-3) kg, sugarcane (6-8) kg, and fruit trees (0.2 to 0.3) kg per plant. When it is found that crops show the symptoms of phosphorus deficiency, phosphorus fertilizer is sprayed on the leaf surface to make phosphorus enter the object through the stomata or cuticle on the leaf surface. Rice, wheat and other gramineous crops can be used at 2% to 3% concentration, rape, vegetables can be used at 1% concentration. It can also use 0.25% concentration of potassium dihydrogen phosphate to spray after the dew dries in the morning or before the dew in the evening, which has a significant increase in production.

Monoammonium Phosphate(MAP 12-61-0) MAP is an efficient source of phosphorus. It facilitates plant uptake of the phosphorus naturally present in the soil. This is due to the ammonium (NH4+) in MAP, which lowers the pH in the root zone and thus enhances phosphorus availability.   Monoammonium phosphate (short for MAP 12-61-0) is a widely used source of phosphorus (P) and nitrogen (N). It's made of two constituents common in the fertilizer industry and contains the most phosphorus of any common solid fertilizer. Tech  MAP 12-61-0 is full water soluble fertilizers for nutrigation and foliar feeding of all crops. It is recommended for use at the begining of the growth season, when phosphorus availanbility is crucial for the establishment of the root system and flowering. It can be mixed with other fertilizers to meet crop nutritional needs through out the growth cycle. MAP 12-61-0 is suitable for preparation of fertilizer blends and can be used for production of liquid fertilziers.

urea.content:
WHAT IS DIESEL EXHAUST FLUID (DEF)
Diesel Exhaust Fluid (DEF) is a non-toxic, high purity solution comprised of 32.5% urea and 67.5% de-ionized water for specific use in Selective Catalytic Reduction (SCR) systems. Using this fluid in diesel-powered vehicles, such as trucks, buses, and tractors, helps to reduce the amount of nitrogen oxides (NOx) emitted into the air by 90%. It is not a fuel additive and must be filled into the dedicated Diesel Exhaust Fluid tank on your diesel vehicle.
SCR/DEF grade urea, automotive grade urea high purity
adblue grade urea
Nitrogen: 46.6%
Moisture: 0.35%
Biuret:0.7%
Particle size(0.85-2.80mm): 94%
Urea 46% or Urea N 46% or Urea prilled or Industry Urea
CAS:57-13-6
Molecular Formula:CH4N2Ol
Molecular Weight:60.06
Melting point:132-135C
Relative density:1.335g/ml at 25C(lit)
How Diesel Exhaust Fluid Works When injected into the exhaust stream with a specialized dosing module, DEF vaporizes, leaving amonia molecules en route to the catalytic converter; once there, it converts NOx molecules into harmless nitrogen and water vapor-making for cleaner emissions. Making sure you do not run out of DEF in an SCR equipped truck is simple - watch the DEF gauge, which is part of the diesel fuel gauge, for when it is time to refill. DEF is stored in a dedicated tank next to the fuel tank on the driver's side. The tanks range in size from 6 to 23 gallons depending on the truck's application. Automotive Grade Urea Automotive Grade Urea is a special, uncoated version of highest purity. It is a main component of urea solutions like Diesel Exhaust Fluid or AUS40/45 which are used within SCR-systems for the reason of NOx reduction. It is the only type of Urea that meets the ISO 22241 specification and the only suitable Urea for the production of DEF/AUS32/ARLA32 solutions. For meeting the ISO 22241 the Urea needs to be of highest purity/quality. Specification

Brown Sugar with an ICUMSA (International Commission for Uniform Methods of Sugar Analysis) range of 600-1200 is a type of raw sugar that retains a significant amount of molasses, giving it a rich, caramel-like flavor and a moist texture. This sugar is less refined than white sugar, resulting in a higher content of natural minerals and a darker color. It is widely used in baking, cooking, and various culinary applications where a deep, robust sweetness is desired.

thai-brown-jasmine-rice.content:
Brown Jasmine Rice

While white jasmine rice is the most common variety, brown jasmine rice has been gaining popularity in recent years. As a health-conscious individual, I’ve found brown jasmine rice to be an excellent alternative that doesn’t compromise on flavor or aroma. Brown jasmine rice retains its outer bran layer, which gives it a nuttier flavor and chewier texture compared to its white counterpart. This bran layer also means this  rice is more nutritious, containing higher levels of fiber, vitamins, and minerals. It’s an excellent choice for those looking to incorporate more whole grains into their diet. Cooking brown jasmine rice requires a slightly different approach than white jasmine rice. It typically needs more water and a longer cooking time to achieve the desired tenderness. However, the extra effort is well worth it for the added nutritional benefits and unique flavor profile.

high-oleic-sunflower-oil.title: High Oleic Sunflower Oil
high-oleic-sunflower-oil.excerpt: High Oleic Sunflower Oil is a specially bred form of sunflower oil that is rich in oleic acid, a monounsaturated fat that provides exceptional stability, high heat resistance, and a long shelf...
high-oleic-sunflower-oil.content:
High Oleic Sunflower Oil is a specially bred form of sunflower oil that is rich in oleic acid, a monounsaturated fat that provides exceptional stability, high heat resistance, and a long shelf life. It offers nutritional benefits by increasing "good" HDL cholesterol without raising "bad" LDL cholesterol and is ideal for high-heat cooking methods like frying, as well as for applications in baking, dressings, and even skincare due to its neutral taste and emollient properties.
Key Characteristics
  • High in Oleic Acid: 
    The "high oleic" designation means the oil contains a minimum of 75-80% oleic acid, a monounsaturated omega-9 fatty acid. 
  • High Stability: 
    The high concentration of oleic acid makes the oil significantly more stable and resistant to oxidation compared to conventional sunflower oil. 
  • High Smoke Point: 
    It can withstand high cooking temperatures, making it suitable for frying and other high-heat applications. 
  • Neutral Taste: 
    It has a neutral flavor profile, which allows it to enhance the natural tastes of food without overpowering them. 
  • Trans-Fat Free: 
    It provides a trans-fat-free alternative for food manufacturers and consumers. 
Nutritional Benefits 
  • Cardiovascular Health: Regular consumption of high oleic sunflower oil, especially when replacing saturated fats, may help lower LDL ("bad") cholesterol and increase HDL ("good") cholesterol, supporting heart health.
Uses in Food & Cosmetics
  • High-Heat Cooking: 
    Ideal for frying, sautéing, and roasting due to its stability at high temperatures. 
  • Bakery and Coatings: 
    Used in spray coatings for crackers, cereals, and dried fruits, as well as in bakery items. 
  • Dressings: 
    Can be used in salad dressings and other food products that require a neutral-tasting oil. 
  • Skincare: 
    Its emollient and hydrating properties make it a valuable ingredient in creams, lotions, and other cosmetic formulations.
